results.rendered_html:
aśriḥ -śrī f. [aśyate saṃhanyate anayā aś vaṃkryādi˚ kri; cf.  Uṇ. 4. 137 ]
1 A corner, angle (of a room, house &c.) (changed to aśra at the end of comp. with catur, tri, ṣaṭ and a few other words; see caturasra);
— aṣṭāśrirvai
(pb) vajraḥ  Ait. Br. 
2 The sharp side or edge (of a weapon &c.); vṛtrasya haṃtuḥ kuliśaṃ kuṃṭhitāśrīva lakṣyate  Ku. 2. 20. 
3 The sharp side of anything.
